findings.title: All ETH, extracted profits, and arbitrary ERC20s are permanently locked in the contract

best_agent_match.title: Any ERC20 sent to `FlawVerifier` is permanently unrecoverable

findings.title: Forced ETH donations can permanently brick `executeOnOpportunity`

best_agent_match.title: Any ERC20 sent to `FlawVerifier` is permanently unrecoverable

findings.title: Anyone can permissionlessly trigger the hardcoded exploit once the contract is funded

best_agent_match.title: Hardcoded external addresses make execution unsafe outside the intended deployment environment

rejected_candidates.title: Counter state is globally mutable by any address
rejected_candidates.reason: `Counter.sol` is an isolated toy/example contract with no privileged flows, assets, or integrations in this codebase; unrestricted setters alone do not create realistic protocol-level harm here.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded external addresses make execution unsafe outside the intended deployment environment
rejected_candidates.reason: This is deployment/configuration hygiene rather than a vulnerability in the intended environment; the contract is explicitly hardwired to one pair and one WETH address.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded 0.1 ETH profit floor rejects smaller but still profitable opportunities
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an intentional strategy parameter/tradeoff, not a security flaw; rejecting low-margin opportunities does not itself create theft, insolvency, lockup beyond the already-reported balance-bricking issue, or permissionless exploitation.
